landing.heading: AoO Team Planner
landing.subtitle: Build and share team assignments for Ark of Osiris
landing.createDescription: Start fresh with a new team plan. You'll get a shareable link that anyone can use to view and edit.
landing.step1: Create a plan - Click the button above to start a new team plan
landing.step2: Build your teams - Select players, distribute to zones, assign rally leads
landing.step3: Share the link - Copy the URL and share with your team leaders
landing.step4: Collaborate - Anyone with the link can view and edit the plan
landing.step5: Running a league? - Add the league sign-up tab on import. Its 45 players form a separate fixed-roster team and are excluded from weekend Team 1 / Team 2 sign-ups; lock the roster for the tournament so mid-season sheet edits can't change it.

header.officerLogout: Logged in as officer (click to log out)

collaborative.label: Collaborative Plan

officer.title: Officer Login
officer.description: Enter the officer password to access the registration sheet link.

registration.uploadCsvHint: Export your Google Sheet as CSV (File → Download → Comma-separated values).
registration.sheetFormatDesc: Your sheet should have these columns (order doesn't matter):
registration.booleanHint: Boolean columns use "x" (case-insensitive) to mark true, leave blank for false. Column matching is flexible — headers just need to contain the keyword.

columns.name: Name
columns.confirmedDescFull: Officer-confirmed for participation. Players without an x are not considered for any team.
columns.subDescFull: Slotted as a sub — different in-game role from main, not a synonym for 'maybe'. Locked to the bench at distribution and can be swapped in.
columns.leagueDescFull: Players signed up on the league tab. Excluded from Team 1 / Team 2; play on the dedicated league team.

league.label: League sign-up tab
league.helper: Players signed up here only play on the league team — they're automatically excluded from Team 1 / Team 2 sign-ups on the main tab.
league.placeholder: Paste the URL of the league tab (different gid)
league.loaded: {count, plural, =0 {No league players loaded} one {# league player loaded · excluded from Team 1 / Team 2} other {# league players loaded · excluded from Team 1 / Team 2}}
league.pendingConfirmed: {count, plural, one {# row on the tab is missing a Confirmed or Team 1 mark — they won't be routed into the league team until both are checked} other {# rows on the tab are missing a Confirmed or Team 1 mark — they won't be routed into the league team until both are checked}}

tournament.frozenSummary: {count, plural, one {# player frozen} other {# players frozen}}
tournament.ignoredUntilEnded: sheet edits are ignored until ended
tournament.lockButton: Lock the {count}-player roster for a tournament
tournament.lockTitle: Snapshot the current league players. While the tournament is active, mid-tournament sheet edits are ignored.
tournament.namePrompt: Name this tournament (e.g. "Spring 2026 League"). The current league players will be frozen until you end the tournament — even if the sheet is edited later.
tournament.endConfirm: End "{name}"? The league team will go back to reading from the live sheet tab.

drift.title: League sheet differs from locked roster
drift.explanation: These changes are ignored — the planner uses the locked roster.

registration.noRegistrations: No registrations found. Check that the sheet has the expected columns.

league.tabTitle: League team — fixed roster, weekly main/sub split
league.countTitle: Mains playing this weekend / Subs on the bench
league.mainTitle: Currently a main this weekend — click to bench (mark as sub). Subs are locked to the bench at distribution.
league.subTitle: Currently benched as sub this weekend — click to put back in the lineup. Subs are locked to the bench at distribution.

builder.addMoreTeams: (Use team count buttons to add more teams)
builder.hintSelect: Mark players as <confirmed>✓ Confirmed</confirmed> or <maybe>? Maybe</maybe> for each team, then distribute to lanes below.
builder.hintDistribute: Assign leads, toggle ⚡ teleport first, and move players between lanes. When done, click Apply All Teams.
builder.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or governor ID...
builder.addMemberHint: Start typing to search existing roster, or enter a new name.
builder.name: Name
builder.noPlayersHint: Use the <confirmed>✓</confirmed> and <maybe>?</maybe> buttons above to add players to a team.
builder.substitutesHint: Players marked as "Maybe" - move to a lane if they confirm
builder.benchHint: Registered but not in the active roster (30 lanes + 10 subs). Move to a lane or sub slot if a spot opens.
builder.benchHintLeague: Registered but not in the active league roster (30 lanes + 15 subs). Move to a lane or sub slot if a spot opens.
builder.coordinatorsHint: Click to toggle coordinator permissions. Rally/garrison leads are good candidates.
builder.needPlayers: Need at least 1 player to distribute across all teams
builder.failedSave: Failed to save pending members. They will still be available in this session.
builder.failedCreateGeneric: Failed to create plan. Please try again.
builder.planNotFound: Plan not found. It may have been deleted.

config.title: Scoring Configuration
config.sandboxHint: Edits are local-only — they won't affect what others see.
config.deployHint: Publish your current edits to the shared kingdom database — every viewer will see them on next refresh.
config.discardHint: Throw away your local edits and revert to the published config.
config.resetHint: Revert your local edits back to the settings officers have deployed.
config.splitToggleHint: Use two separate weight sets — one for accounts under the power threshold, one for accounts at or above. Lets you score smaller accounts differently than whales.
config.howItWorksLead: Each category is scored 0–100 relative to the top player in the kingdom for that category.
config.howItWorksDetails: DKP is computed from the formula (T4/T5 kills + deaths). Then DKP, RSS, helps and honor are each divided by the kingdom's max in that category (top player = 100). Those four sub-scores are blended using the weights to produce a final 0–100 number, which maps to a status tier. Expected KP is independent and only affects the KP cell color. Hover any

dkpFormulaCard.title: DKP Formula
dkpFormulaCard.hint: How the raw DKP number is built from each player's T4/T5 kills and deaths. Higher coefficients reward that activity more.

expectedKpCard.title: Expected KP
expectedKpCard.hint: The KP each player is expected to produce based on their power. Smaller accounts use the low multiplier, larger accounts the high one. KP performance (actual KP ÷ target KP) feeds into the Score Weights as the 'KP' weight.
expectedKpCard.smallerHint: For accounts under {threshold}M power, target KP = power × this. Default 3 means a 30M player should hit 90M KP.
expectedKpCard.largerHint: For accounts at or above {threshold}M power, target KP = power × this. Default 10 means a 60M player should hit 600M KP.
expectedKpCard.thresholdNote: (shared with the Score Weights split toggle).

readingTableCard.title: Reading the Table
readingTableCard.hint: Quick reference for what each column and color in the player table means.
readingTableCard.colTargetKpDesc: — power × multiplier (×3 small / ×10 large by default)
readingTableCard.colDkpDesc: — weighted kills + deaths from the formula
readingTableCard.colScoreDesc: — 0–100, weighted blend of how this player ranks vs the kingdom max in each category

weightsCard.title: Score Weights
weightsCard.hint: How much each sub-score contributes to the final number. Values are relative — the badge on each band shows what share each weight effectively gets.
weightsCard.shareHint: Effective share of each weight after dividing by the total

cutoffsCard.title: Status Cutoffs
cutoffsCard.hint: The minimum final score (0–100) needed to land in each tier. Anything below the GOOD cutoff falls into REVIEW (still listed, just flagged for officer attention).

weights.dkpHint: Combined kills + deaths score using the formula coefficients on the left. Higher kills + deaths during KvK = higher DKP.
weights.rssHint: Resources gathered from the kingdom map (food, wood, stone, gold). Rewards active gathering.
weights.helpsHint: Alliance helps given. Rewards being active in the alliance and supporting teammates.
weights.honorHint: Honor points earned (PvP, events). Imported from the Statmaster honor rankings file.

formula.t4KillHint: Points awarded for each Tier 4 kill made during KvK.
formula.t5KillHint: Points awarded for each Tier 5 kill. T5 are the strongest troops, so usually weighted higher than T4 kills.
formula.t4DeathHint: Points awarded for each Tier 4 death (sacrifice). Deaths typically count more than kills since they cost the player real troops.
formula.t5DeathHint: Points awarded for each Tier 5 death. Usually the highest coefficient in the formula since T5 sacrifices are the most costly.

cutoffHints.excellent: Top tier — players whose final score is at or above this threshold are marked EXCELLENT.
cutoffHints.approved: Players hitting at least this threshold are STRONG (clearly pulling weight, just below the top tier).
cutoffHints.good: Players hitting at least this threshold are GOOD (acceptable). Below this they fall into REVIEW.
cutoffHints.rejected: Anything below the GOOD threshold lands here. Flagged for officer attention rather than auto-rejected.

columns.playerHint: In-game username from the kingdom export.
columns.powerHint: Current power as of the last upload (not highest power).
columns.t4KpHint: T4 kill points from the kingdom export.
columns.t5KpHint: T5 kill points from the kingdom export.
columns.totalKpHint: Actual total kill points from the kingdom export. Cell is colored green if this player meets or beats their Target KP, red if they fall short.
columns.targetKpHint: KP this player is expected to produce, based on their power. Smaller accounts use the low multiplier, larger accounts the high one (configured in Expected KP).
columns.t4DeathsHint: T4 troop deaths from the kingdom export.
columns.t5DeathsHint: T5 troop deaths from the kingdom export.
columns.dkpHint: Raw DKP for this player from the formula in the config panel (T4/T5 kills + T4/T5 deaths weighted).
columns.scoreHint: Final 0–100 score. Each of DKP, RSS, helps and honor is scored 0–100 relative to the top player in the kingdom for that category, then blended using the score weights. 100 = top player in every weighted category.
columns.statusHint: Tier the score lands in (EXCELLENT / STRONG / GOOD / REVIEW).
columns.honorHint: Raw honor points from the Statmaster honor file (matched by name).

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or Gov ID…
filters.rankTooltip: Global rank by current sort. Search/filter does not renumber.

explainer.title: How "Vs Model Player" works
explainer.subtitle: The fair way to compare a 25M-power scout to an 87M-power whale.
explainer.step1Body: Whales and farms get judged differently. Each player lands in one of three groups based on their current power.
explainer.step1Footnote: The 42M cutoff comes from the power threshold in the scoring config. Change it there and the bands move with it.
explainer.step2Title: Pick the "model player" for each band
explainer.step2BodyB: of players by performance and grab the
explainer.step2BodyC: of their stats. That median becomes the typical "strong" player for that band — the bar everyone else gets compared to.
explainer.step2ExampleLabel: Example: a strong T4 might look like
explainer.step3Title: Show each player as a ratio
explainer.step3BodyA: In this view every stat cell becomes
explainer.step3BodyB: . A 1.42× means "42% above the typical strong player in your band."
explainer.step4BodyB: who deals 100M DKP looks weak next to an
explainer.step4BodyC: doing 1B DKP. On the kingdom-wide score, the scout is buried.
explainer.step4BodyF: — they're crushing it for their size class. This view surfaces them.
explainer.headsUpBody: the status tier (EXCELLENT/STRONG/GOOD/REVIEW) still uses the kingdom-wide score so cutoffs stay consistent. The Score column shows both numbers in this view. Bands and the model auto-recalculate any time officers change weights or the formula.

upload.heading: Upload new dataset
upload.noticeShared: Uploads are saved to the shared kingdom database — everyone sees the new dataset immediately.
upload.noticeDateRange: and end on the most recent day available.

guide.title: How Scoring Works
guide.subtitle: A complete guide to how every player's score and status are calculated.
guide.step1Title: Players are grouped into power bands
guide.step1Body: Each player falls into one of three bands based on their current power. A {mt4} is never compared against a {t5} whale — they're in different leagues.
guide.step2Title: Each band has its own scoring formula
guide.step2Body: 7 components: T4 Kills, T5 Kills, T4 Deaths, T5 Deaths, RSS, Helps, Honor. Each gets a weight from 0–100. Weights are relative — they don't need to sum to 100. A weight of 50 vs 25 just means that stat counts twice as much. Set a weight to 0 to ignore that stat entirely (e.g. T5 Deaths = 0 for mT4 since they can't field T5 troops).
guide.step3Title: Stats are normalized within the band
guide.step3Body: For each stat, the top player in that band = 100. Everyone else scores proportionally. This is what makes it fair — a mT4 with 50M T4 kills gets judged against other mT4s, not against a whale doing 500M.
guide.step4Body: All 7 normalized sub-scores are blended using the band's weights. Result is a single 0–100 number shown in the Score column. The small gray (k X.X) next to it is the same math but against the whole kingdom — for reference only.
guide.step5Body: Each band has its own cutoffs. If a player's band score hits the threshold, they get that tier. A top mT4 can hit EXCELLENT for being the best mT4 — they don't compete against whales.
guide.kpNote: KP Target is independent from the score. It sets an expected KP based on power × a multiplier. The KP cell turns green (≥100%), amber (80–99%), or red (<80%) based on how the player's actual KP compares to that target. It does not affect the score or status.

bandColumn.kpTargetHelp: Expected KP = power × this multiplier. Colors the KP cell — does not affect the score.
bandColumn.kpTargetHint: Target KP = power × this. {example}M player → {target}M KP.
bandColumn.formulaHelp: Weights are relative (0–100). They don't need to add to 100 — only the ratio matters.
bandColumn.cutoffsHelp: Minimum band score needed for each tier. Applied to this band only.

formulaMeta.t4KillHint: Tier 4 kill points. Set to 0 if you don't want this band judged on T4 kills.
formulaMeta.t5KillHint: Tier 5 kill points. Usually 0 for mT4 since smaller accounts can't earn these.
formulaMeta.t4DeathHint: Tier 4 deaths (sacrifice). Rewards taking hits, not just dealing them.
formulaMeta.t5DeathHint: Tier 5 deaths. Usually 0 for mT4 since smaller accounts can't field T5 troops.
formulaMeta.rssHint: Resources gathered from the kingdom map. Rewards active gathering.
formulaMeta.helpsHint: Alliance helps given. Rewards being active in the alliance.
formulaMeta.honorHint: Honor points earned (PvP, events). From the Statmaster honor rankings file.
